title: Lesson 6: Meet your AI pair programmer

steps.text:
You've been typing every command by hand. That's good — you
needed to understand what the terminal does. Now you're going
to use an AI tool called Claude Code that can write code for
you, right here in your terminal.

## What Claude Code is

Claude Code is an AI assistant that lives in your terminal.
You type instructions in plain English, and it writes code,
creates files, and runs commands. Think of it as a very fast
coworker who needs clear directions.

First, let's set up Claude Code with some rules for this
lesson so it knows to guide you as a beginner.

Important: when Claude Code is running, it takes over
your terminal. You won't be able to see these instructions
while Claude is active.

Here's what to do inside Claude Code:

1. Replace the content of index.html with a simple web page
   that has a heading saying "Welcome to Claudzooks" and
   a paragraph saying "I built this with AI."

2. Add a second paragraph: "This is my first project."

3. Change the heading color to blue using a style tag.

Read what Claude explains — that's how you learn what the
code means.

To come back here, type /exit or press Ctrl-C.

Ready? Let's start Claude Code:

Welcome back! Let's see what Claude built.
Open your page in the browser:

## What you learned
- Claude Code runs in your terminal with the `claude` command
- You give it instructions in plain English
- It explains what it's doing before making changes
- Type `/exit` or press Ctrl-C to leave Claude Code
- You can check results by opening files in your browser
